title: Allow relative durations/dependencies in templates so timelines auto‑schedule from project start

posts.author: amanda.kelly (Amanda Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Summary
Enable board templates to store duration (in days) for each item and define dependencies between items. When a user creates a new board from that template and provides a single project start date (or uses the creation date), monday.com should auto‑generate each item’s Timeline/Date based on duration and dependency logic—no manual date entry required.

What are you trying to achieve

Dramatically reduce setup time for templated, repeatable workflows.
Prevent scheduling errors and improve adoption of Dependencies/Gantt.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Amanda, just to make sure I’m understanding correctly. Are you asking for templates to support relative scheduling, where you could:

  • define durations (number of days) in the template

  • set up dependencies between items

  • then when a new board is created and a project start date is entered, the timelines automatically generate based on those rules?

So essentially the timelines would auto-schedule from a single start date using the dependencies and durations defined in the template.

Just want to confirm that’s the behavior you’re looking for.

I ask because based on the current documentation for Managed templates, some of the column settings needed for that type of scheduling are either partially supported or not supported when syncing to template instances.

For example:

  • Timeline Column: settings like connecting to a Duration column or setting deadlines do not transfer

  • Dependency Column: column settings are not currently supported

So just confirming that the feature request is specifically about supporting Timeline + Dependency logic in templates so schedules can cascade automatically from a single start date.
